repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
<!--
  Thank you for contributing to Neovim!
  If this is your first time, check out https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests-prs
  for our PR guidelines.
-->

## Problem
`vim_getenv` is followed by `TO_SLASH` when getting path-related variables.
cmd exits when launched with forward slash.

## Solution
try calling `TO_SLASH` in `vim_getenv`.
only include `cmd.exe` in cmdline.

Continue #39382

comment_data.body: `argc == 0 ? "cmd.exe"` seems strange since `is_cmdexe` was already checked above
`is_cmdexe` is used to distinguish `cmd.exe` from other programs' arg quoting logic, and it doesn't change during iteration. We only need to replace the first arg, all other args remain unchanged. :)

I've been torn between adding a param or just making some common vars to always return `/`. e.g. `HOME/VIM/VIMRUNTIME/PATH/CDPATH/TMPDIR/TMP/TEMP/MYVIMRC`. the latter would give users consistent `/` when using `$PATH / getenv('PATH') / vim.env.PATH`
Let's try the "common vars" approach, since that is the remit of `vim_getenv` anyway.

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -872,6 +871,18 @@ char *vim_getenv(const char *name)
 #endif
 
   char *kos_env_path = os_getenv(name);
+#ifdef BACKSLASH_IN_FILENAME
+  if (STRICMP(name, "VIMRUNTIME") == 0

comment_data.body:
if this is about cmd.exe let's be clear. also, line length max is 100...

```suggestion
  // cmd.exe can’t recognize forward slash in argv[0] during
```

should we use `snprintf` to improve the message?

    "not executable: \"%s\""

will add this ref https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/msrc/blog/2014/04/ms14-019-fixing-a-binary-hijacking-via-cmd-or-bat-file, it suggests passing fully qualified cmd.exe path.
I’ll include details in the future to avoid any confusion. :D

```
ELOG("pty_proc_spawn(%s): %s: error code: %d", proc->argv[0], emsg, status);
pty_proc_spawn(cmd.exe): file is not executable: error code: -4058`
```

`cmd.exe` also appears inside the parentheses. How about `executable not found`? I’m not exactly sure what the most natural way to phrase it is, my English isn’t great. I run every sentence through GPT for translation (sometimes even multiple AIs, also learn by talking to them) :D

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -872,6 +871,18 @@ char *vim_getenv(const char *name)
 #endif
 
   char *kos_env_path = os_getenv(name);
+#ifdef BACKSLASH_IN_FILENAME
+  if (striequal(name, "VIMRUNTIME") == 0

comment_data.body: Ah, sorry for the silly mistake... 😅

comment_regular_data.body: restarted ci, i think that was just a flaky test.
I plan to keep `HOME` env and other test changes in PR #39155. :)
